name: Daedalus
description: Daedalus was a legendary architect, inventor, and craftsman in Greek mythology. Renowned for his exceptional skill and ingenuity, he was credited with creating numerous inventions, including the Labyrinth for King Minos of Crete. His most famous creation was the set of wings he fashioned for himself and his son, Icarus, to escape Crete after falling out of favor with Minos. Daedalus's story is a cautionary tale about the dangers of hubris and the limitations of human ambition, as Icarus perished when he flew too close to the sun, melting the wax that held his wings together. Despite this tragedy, Daedalus's legacy as a master craftsman and inventor endures, representing the human desire to overcome limitations and explore new possibilities through innovation. He also built a wooden cow for Pasiphae, allowing her to mate with a bull and give birth to the Minotaur. Daedalus's skills were sought after by many rulers, but his restless nature and tendency to become entangled in political intrigue often led to conflict and exile. His journey represents the constant struggle between creation and destruction, innovation and consequence.
subtitle: Master Craftsman and Ingenious Inventor
achievements: Building the wooden cow for Pasiphae

keyMyths.title: The Labyrinth
keyMyths.description: Daedalus constructed the Labyrinth to contain the Minotaur. It was so complex that Daedalus himself barely escaped after its completion, highlighting the brilliance and danger of his creation.

keyMyths.title: The Wings of Icarus
keyMyths.description: To escape Crete, Daedalus fashioned wings of feathers and wax for himself and Icarus. He warned Icarus not to fly too close to the sun, but the young man disobeyed, leading to his tragic demise.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Perdix
keyMyths.description: Daedalus, jealous of his nephew Perdix's talent, murdered him. He was then exiled from Athens for his crime, setting him on the path to Crete and King Minos.

sources.author: Apollodorus

sources.text: A comprehensive source of Greek myths and legends.
